## [03:06 - 06:08]

expect. So I'm looking at about 1% as my benchmark for this particular campaign because I know what the website conversion performance looks like. And so my page first engagement, if I get that kind of level of performance from a PPE ad, PPE ad, I know that when I move into WC, it's going to probably do as well. And that's all part of this whole test process. However, what I want to also look at is the cost per click as well, because that's the ratio of the CPM that you're paying and the click for rate that you're getting. And this particular one is doing really well, 0.41 on my cost per click. And also I'm looking at cost per landing page view as well. And just to iterate here that a link click from your ad doesn't mean that they've hit your landing page. So I'm monitoring that as well. Off the landing page is straight into purchase. So there is no cars. There's no other metric that I'm looking at down the funnel. So really what I'm looking at as a hint here is what is my CPC and my cost per landing page view. And you can clearly see out of all of these, this particular audience here is doing really well. And so I'm really happy for this one to be moving over into my website conversion campaign. Now, how do you do that? There's probably different ways that you might think about doing it, maybe recreate it in your website conversion campaign. But actually, with the new ads manager, there is something good about it. You can do a really fast duplication, you can move it from your PPE to your WC. So if you select existing, look for your WC campaign, which for me is this one here. And then you literally just make a duplicate. Now, if I wanted to, I might make two or three duplicates, especially when you move an audience over from your testing into your main campaign, there's no guarantee that it's going to find the best audience straight away. So going back to understanding oceans, boats and hooks, sometimes when you drop an ad set in, it just lands about audience and you have to then pause it and start again. So in this example, I'm going to actually launch two versions of exactly the same audience, because I want to make sure that the audience works. So I'm going to launch it into my WC. I'm going to give it a small amount of spend, and then come back and analyze it. And hopefully, at least one of them does well, if both of them do well, perfect, I'll let them both run. If one does bad, one does okay, I'll pause one, let the other one go. If both do bad, then I might restart that again, knowing that my graduation worked well. So let's go ahead and just duplicate that. So what this will do is it will take the copy of this ad set and move it into my main campaign. So it's a super easy way of getting that set up. And so it gives you the options if you want to edit it. One thing to note, especially when you go from PPE to website conversion is there'll be no pixel event because PPE doesn't have one.
